title: Silent 'H' and Intento
description: Learn about the silent 'h' in Spanish and how to use 'intento' for expressing 'I try'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Intento means 'I try' or 'I am trying'. How would you say 'I am trying to publish it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Intento publicarlo. How would you say 'I am trying to administrate it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Intento administrarlo. How would you say 'I am trying to imitate it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Exactly. Now, remember that h's in Spanish are completely silent. How would you say 'hospital'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Perfect. Hospital. How would you say 'horrible'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Now, how would you say 'habitual'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Habitualmente. The word for 'now' in Spanish is 'ahora'. How would you say it?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Ahora' is spelled a-h-o-r-a. How would you say 'I want to cancel it, but I don’t want to cancel it now'?

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: quiero cancelarlo, pero no quiero cancelarlo ahora

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Perfect! The word for 'I go' or 'I am going' in Spanish is 'voy'. How would you say 'I am going now'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Voy ahora. How would you say 'I am not going now'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How do you think you might say 'visit' in Spanish?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'I want to visit him'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How do you think you would say 'visible' in Spanish?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Viral. How do you think 'virus' might be?

outro.text: You've learned how to use 'intento' to express 'I try' and the importance of the silent 'h' in Spanish.
Try using 'intento' with other verbs you know. How would you say 'I am trying to learn'?
Remember, the 'h' is silent in Spanish. Practice saying words like 'hospital' and 'horrible' without pronouncing the 'h'.
